query: Miller v Nelson Texas ballot access Fifth Circuit 2024 independent candidate signature requirements ruling
answer: **Summary:** I was unable to retrieve specific details about the *Miller v. Nelson* Fifth Circuit ruling due to all web research tools being unavailable in this session. I have provided comprehensive background on Texas's independent candidate ballot access statutory framework (§§ 142.002, 142.004, 142.006), the applicable constitutional standard (Anderson-Burdick), and the Fifth Circuit's general jurisprudential posture on these issues. I have **not** fabricated any case-specific details (holding, citation, panel, dates) that I cannot verify. I recommend checking CourtListener, Ballot Access News, or Google Scholar Case Law for the specific opinion.
key_facts: Texas Election Code § 142.002 requires independent candidates to gather petition signatures equal to 1% of total votes cast in the last gubernatorial election
Texas Election Code § 142.004 imposes a 'primary participation bar' — petition signers must not have voted in any party primary that cycle
Texas Election Code § 142.006 sets early filing deadlines that compress the signature-gathering window for independent candidates
The Anderson-Burdick balancing framework governs constitutional challenges to ballot access restrictions
The Fifth Circuit has historically been deferential to Texas's ballot access restrictions, generally upholding signature requirements
Texas's combination of high signature thresholds, primary voter exclusion, and early deadlines makes it one of the most restrictive states for independent candidate ballot access
CRITICAL LIMITATION: Web search tools were unavailable; specific details of Miller v. Nelson (citation, holding, panel, date) could not be verified and are not reported to avoid fabrication

data_points.metric: Texas independent candidate petition signature threshold (% of last gubernatorial vote)
